source.source_section: Diabetes Insipidus generated endocrine workup: diagnostic frame
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

differentialBuckets.action: Use this frame to decide whether Confirm hypotonic polyuria: adult urine volume often >3 L/day or >40-50 mL/kg/day with urine osmolality often <300 mOsm/kg. is the right first confirmatory step and whether urgent endocrine escalation is needed.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Provides diagnostic or safety thresholds that change interpretation and management for Diabetes Insipidus.

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia. Urine osmolality <300 mOsm/kg is hypotonic; >800 often argues against DI.

differentialBuckets.action: Use local assay and patient context; do not apply numeric anchors without clinical interpretation.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Provides diagnostic or safety thresholds that change interpretation and management for Diabetes Insipidus.

differentialBuckets.label: Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants.

source.source_section: Diabetes Insipidus generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia. Urine osmolality <300 mOsm/kg is hypotonic; >800 often argues against DI. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
initialTests.rationale: Provides diagnostic or safety thresholds that change interpretation and management for Diabetes Insipidus.
